items: Drafting: CAD software helps designers create, modify, analyze, and document precise designs.
Electronics: Binary represents information with two states, commonly written as 0 and 1.
Emergency Preparedness: If clothing catches fire, stop, drop, and roll; then cool burns with clean running water.
Energy: Devices work by converting energy from one form into another.
Engineering: Professional engineers are expected to put public safety, honesty, and competence first.
Entrepreneurship: A workable business needs planning, customer knowledge, financing, and clear communication.
Environmental Science: Pollination moves pollen between flower parts and enables many plants to produce seeds and fruit.
Exploration: The crewed submersible Alvin can carry scientists thousands of meters beneath the ocean surface.
Family Life: Empathy means understanding another person’s experience from their point of view.
Fingerprinting: Fingerprints remain one of the most widely used forms of biometric identification.
Fire Safety: Class K extinguishers are designed for fires involving commercial cooking oils and fats.
First Aid: Gloves and other barriers reduce exposure to blood and other potentially infectious material.
Fish and Wildlife Management: Creel surveys ask anglers about effort and catch to help managers understand fisheries.
Fishing: A fish’s mucus coating helps protect its skin from injury, parasites, and infection.
Forestry: Wide and narrow tree rings can record changing growing conditions from year to year.
Game Design: A vision statement keeps the game’s central idea clear while details change.
Gardening: Vermicomposting uses worms and microorganisms to turn food scraps into soil amendment.
Genealogy: Census, military, immigration, and vital records can help reconstruct family history.
Geocaching: Trackables carry unique codes so their journeys can be logged from cache to cache.
Geology: A mineral is naturally occurring, inorganic, solid, and has an ordered internal structure.
Golf: The USGA and The R&A jointly maintain the Rules of Golf.
Hiking: Desert biological soil crust is alive and can take years to recover from a single footprint.
Home Repairs: Everyone in a household should know how to reach the main electrical panel safely.
Horsemanship: A horse’s feed must match its body condition, workload, age, and health.
American Indian Culture: Navajo Code Talkers used their language to create secure military communications in World War II.
